text.title: PLG Optimization Tactics Failure Modes
text.summary: Six antipatterns observed when LLMs apply PLG tactics catalogs. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Auto-generated upgrade copy drifts to "powerful features", "enterprise-grade", "premium experience". A/B variants are all interchangeable.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM falls back to high-frequency training tokens for upgrade contexts.
antipattern.fix: Post-filter the generator against the banned-phrase list; auto-reject + regenerate when the named-customer + quantified-value pattern is absent.

antipattern.symptom: Agent recommends "remove all limits" or "extend trial to 60 days" to boost activation.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM optimizes apparent activation by removing friction, ignoring the upgrade-trigger half of the balance.
antipattern.fix: Pin the balanced-free-tier rule in the system prompt; require every limit-change tactic to name the upgrade-trigger it preserves.

antipattern.symptom: Agent ships a free-tier limit change directly because its ICE score is high.
antipattern.root-cause: Free-tier limits look like an in-product config but couple to revenue + retention.
antipattern.fix: Gate every free-tier limit mutation behind finance + retention sign-off; agents emit a PR/ticket, never a live change.

antipattern.symptom: Generated Appcues / Pendo flow triggers the upgrade prompt at exactly the limit (100% usage), not before.
antipattern.root-cause: Default flow templates use 100% triggers; the LLM copies the default without checking.
antipattern.fix: Generated flows MUST set the trigger at ≤80% and include a unit test of the trigger condition.

antipattern.symptom: Upgrade prompt fires on first session because the agent omits the session-count gate.
antipattern.root-cause: Session-count is a behavioral signal the LLM doesn't naturally encode.
antipattern.fix: Generated flows MUST include `session_count >= 2` (or equivalent) as a hard precondition; validator rejects flows missing this gate.

antipattern.symptom: PQL scoring weights from last quarter's coefficients reused without re-fitting against current converted cohorts.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM reuses the previously-seen weights as if they are constants.
antipattern.fix: Require the agent to re-fit weights against the most recent converted cohort each quarter; record the fit date in the backlog.
